steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice most effectively uses both sets of notes. Masters et al. shows farm sizes rising across Asia, supporting the farm-size transition hypothesis. Promkhambut et al. shows Thai rice farms stayed small despite modernization. The correct answer must draw on both findings.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — hypothesis is unsupported: contradicts Masters et al., which shows rising farm sizes in Asia. ✗
  • Choice B — hypothesis fails everywhere in Asia: relies only on Promkhambut et al. and ignores the broader Asian trend from Masters et al. ✗
  • Choice C — hypothesis holds everywhere: omits the Thai counterexample from Promkhambut et al. ✗
  • Choice D — hypothesis may apply to some Asian countries but is inconsistent with Thailand: synthesizes both notes — affirms the Masters et al. pattern while acknowledging the Promkhambut et al. exception. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
